metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4150
metadata.comment:
Rationale: To study the protein expression pattern and subcellular localization of TBC1D24 / Skywalker (short name Sky), authors created a genomic EGFP-tagged construct (GFP-Sky+) (Figure S1E). The presence of this construct rescues sky related phenotypes (Figure 1C), indicating that GFP-Sky is functional. GFP-Sky appears enriched at synaptic areas in the ventral nerve cord (VNC) of third instar larvae (L3) (Figure 1E). Authors also observe abundant labeling at neuromuscular junction (NMJ) boutons where Sky is present both pre- and postsynaptically (Figure 1H-M). Additionally, an antibody raised against full length HIS-Sky also labels the VNC neuropile (Figure 1F) and NMJ boutons, and this staining is strongly reduced in sky hypomorphic mutants (more than 2-fold), indicating the labeling is specific (Figure 1G).

Rationale for cytosolic localization is inferred from protein properties, as it does not present any transmembrane domains.

14/01/2021 Dnyanada: The paper demonstrates the localisation of GFP-TBC1D24 in the NMJ. However, the images do not provide sufficient resolution to distinguish between pre or postsynaptic localisation. Besides, the localisation is based on overexpression of the protein. Based on these two points, "synapse" was chosen as the appropriate ontology term.. Experimental description: "Larvae were grown on grape juice plates with fresh yeast paste", "All fly stocks were kept on standard corn meal and molasses medium at room temperature", "In this work, we present evidence that (...) Skywalker (Sky) facilitates endosomal trafficking of synaptic vesicles at Drosophila neuromuscular junction boutons"
